title: Standard market simulation
summary: Run a market simulation against one or more scenarios and return shares, revenue, volume, and other measures per product per subgroup.
description: Executes the choice simulator for the named scenarios and returns a results table with one row per scenario × subgroup × product combination. All output measures are computed from the HB individual-level utility draws stored in the sim_db data sheet. The sim_db Excel workbook defines products, attributes, scenarios, respondent data, and optional subgroup and weight columns; this command runs those definitions through the simulator without additional parameter sweeps.
usage:
[sim_db]
excel = "model.xlsx"

[["cs::sim_run"]]
scenarios = ["base"]
measures = "share|revenue"
# optional:
subgroups = ["total"]
weights = ["w1"]
include_nets = false
exponents = 1.0
prob_methods = "sop"
trips = 1

["cs::sim_run".scenario_chgs.ProductA]
price = 9.99

arguments.name: measures
arguments.description: Pipe-separated output measures. Any subset of: share, revenue, volume, cost, profit, standard_error. Use "__all__" for all six. Example: "share|revenue|volume".

arguments.name: scenarios
arguments.description: Names of scenarios (sim_db sheets) to run. Use ["__all__"] to run every scenario defined in the sim_db. When omitted, the sim_db default scenario list is used.

arguments.name: subgroups
arguments.description: Column names in the respondent data to use as subgroup cuts. Use ["__all__"] for every subgroup column in the sim_db. When omitted, only the total (no subgroup split) is returned.

arguments.name: weights
arguments.description: Weight column names. Use ["__all__"] to run with every weight defined in the sim_db. When omitted, the sim_db default weight is used.

arguments.name: throttles
arguments.description: Throttle-set name(s) to apply. Throttles zero out products from the consideration set for selected respondents. Omit to use the sim_db default.

arguments.name: considerations
arguments.description: Consideration-set name(s) to apply. Limits which products each respondent considers. Omit to use the sim_db default.

arguments.name: include_nets
arguments.description: When true, append net rows that sum shares across products sharing the same product_net group label in the sim_db. Useful for brand-level roll-ups.

arguments.name: exponents
arguments.description: Multiplier applied to all beta coefficients before the share-of-preference exponentiation step. Values > 1 sharpen preference; values < 1 soften it. Equivalent to scaling the model scale parameter.

arguments.name: prob_methods
arguments.description: Probability computation method(s). Single method or a pipe-separated blend (e.g. "sop|top2"). Methods: "sop" — standard share-of-preference (multinomial logit); "topN" where N ≥ 1 — top-N restricted MNL, only the N highest-utility products receive non-zero probability ("top1" is equivalent to first choice); "<top_spec>:<bot_spec>-<att>" — two-level nested logit where products are grouped by scenario attribute <att>, with <bot_spec> within groups and <top_spec> across groups (e.g. "sop:sop-Brand", "top1:sop-Category"); "<bot_spec>-<att>" shorthand sets top_spec=sop implicitly (e.g. "sop-Brand"). Blend methods with | and weights via prob_methods_wgts.

arguments.name: prob_methods_wgts
arguments.description: Blend weights corresponding to each entry in prob_methods (when prob_methods is a pipe-separated list). Weights are normalised to sum to 1 before blending.

arguments.name: trips
arguments.description: Number of Monte Carlo shopping trips per respondent. Values > 1 simulate multi-trip purchase behaviour; each trip draws a first-choice product according to prob_methods. Must be >= 1.

arguments.name: trip_seeds
arguments.description: Random seed for Monte Carlo trip simulation. Integer >= 0. Changing this while holding trips fixed produces different but equally valid stochastic draws.

arguments.name: price_formulas
arguments.description: Eval-lang expressions that define derived price attributes for each product. Evaluated before the share computation. Enables relative pricing rules such as "ProductB.price = ProductA.price * 0.9".

arguments.name: return_debug
arguments.description: When true, include per-respondent utility and probability output in the result. Produces much larger output files; use only for diagnostics.

arguments.name: scenario_chgs
arguments.description: Attribute overrides to apply on top of the scenario definition. Keyed by product_name; each value is an object mapping attribute name to a number (single override) or array of numbers (sweep values). For sim_run, only the first value of any array is used — use sim_sensitivities for sweeps.

parameters.name: price
parameters.description: Override price for this product in all run scenarios. Replaces the sim_db value.

parameters.name: units
parameters.description: Override units-per-purchase for this product (used when measures includes volume).

parameters.name: cost
parameters.description: Override unit cost for this product (used when measures includes profit).

parameters.name: share_target
parameters.description: Target market share for this product, used by downstream calibration. Range 0..1.

parameters.name: availability
parameters.description: Respondent-level availability multiplier for this product. 0 = unavailable, 1 = always available.

arguments.name: threads
arguments.description: Rayon worker threads for the scenarios × weights fan-out. `1` (default) runs serially. Each (scenario, weight) pair is scored independently, so parallel speedup scales with their product. Results are deterministic regardless of thread count.

details:
The simulator loads the sim_db from the Excel workbook, reads the HB utility draws from the data sheet, and applies the scenario attribute values on top of the base design. For each respondent, product utilities are computed as the dot product of beta draws and attribute values (with optional exponent scaling), then converted to probabilities using the specified prob_methods.

The output CSV has columns: scenario, subgroup, product, measure, value, error. Measure is one of the requested measures (share, revenue, volume, cost, profit, standard_error); error is the across-draw standard deviation of the measure.

scenario_chgs overrides apply a fixed offset or replacement to specified attribute values for named products across all scenarios in the run. For per-scenario overrides that vary across scenarios, define separate scenarios in the sim_db instead.

When trips > 1, each respondent makes that many independent first-choice purchases, and shares are the proportion of trips captured per product. The random draws are seeded by trip_seeds for reproducibility.
value: Writes one CSV file to the output directory, named sim_run_0.csv, containing all requested measures. In protocol mode the plugin emits event.run.started, an event.artifact.created for the CSV, and event.run.finished.
notes: Use scenarios = ["__all__"] to run every scenario defined in the sim_db without listing them explicitly.
The standard_error measure reports the cross-draw standard deviation of share, which reflects model uncertainty — not sampling variability.
exponents > 1 amplifies preference separation (IIA sensitivity); values near 0 approach equal shares. Use sim_exponent_search to calibrate.
price_formulas are evaluated in sim_db attribute space and can reference other products' attributes by product_name.attribute_name.

see_also.description: Sweep one attribute at a time across products.
Grid or zip search over multiple simultaneous attribute changes.
Identical engine; semantically for take-rate (purchase intent) analyses.
Calibrate the IIA exponent to match stated market shares.
Move multiple products together in parallel.

examples.title: All scenarios, all subgroups, with nets
examples.body:
[sim_db]
excel = "model.xlsx"

[["cs::sim_run"]]
scenarios = ["__all__"]
subgroups = ["__all__"]
measures = "share|volume|revenue"
include_nets = true

examples.title: Nested logit by brand (SOP within and across)

fields.name: scenarios
fields.description: Scenario name(s) to run. Use ["__all__"] for every scenario.

fields.name: subgroups
fields.description: Subgroup column name(s). Use ["__all__"] for all.

fields.name: weights
fields.description: Weight column name(s). Use ["__all__"] for all.

fields.name: throttles
fields.description: Throttle set name(s). Omit to use sim_db default.

fields.name: considerations
fields.description: Consideration set name(s). Omit to use sim_db default.

fields.name: include_nets
fields.description: Add net rows summed by product_net group. Default: false.

fields.name: exponents
fields.description: Beta scale factor applied before exponentiation. Default: 1.0.

fields.name: prob_methods
fields.description: Probability method: sop, topN (N≥1), nested <top>:<bot>-<att>, or pipe-blended. Default: sop.

fields.name: trips
fields.description: Monte Carlo shopping trips per respondent. Default: 1.

fields.name: trip_seeds
fields.description: Random seed for trip simulation. Default: 100.

fields.name: return_debug
fields.description: Include per-respondent debug output. Default: false.

fields.name: threads
fields.description: Rayon worker threads for scenarios × weights fan-out. Default: 1.
